summary: Sprint 12 contract proposal is thorough, well-scoped, and exhaustively testable. It covers all three sprint-plan features (F12 keybinds, F13 audio, F14 daily/leaderboard/badge — plus the run-summary tie-in pulled forward from the deferred Sprint 6) with 20 criteria spanning code-structure, feature, integration, determinism, regression, tests, documentation, code-quality, and behavior categories. Layering invariants and the 'no real audio backend' / 'no network' / 'audio off by default' constraints are explicit. Determinism is rigorously specified across all five deterministic-input modes (--seed, --seed-string, --daily+--daily-date, fixed-timestamp injections). Regression sprints (1, 2, 7, 8, 10, 11) correctly match the in-tree sprints and exclude not-in-tree Sprints 3/4/5/6/9. Out-of-scope list explicitly defers Sprints 3/4/5/6/9, real audio backends, network sync, and 'death legends' (preserved from Sprint 10). The proposal is ACCEPTED.
strengths: Each criterion has multiple concrete testSteps with exact CLI invocations, expected exit codes, expected file contents, and named regex/JSON shapes. A QA evaluator can execute these mechanically.
Layering is enforced via dedicated AST-import checks in C1, C5, C7, C19 — no ambiguity about which modules may import what.
Audio-off-by-default and 'no real audio backend in this sprint' are stated unambiguously (C5, C6, assumptions §4, outOfScope §1). The Protocol+Null+Log shape future-proofs without adding risky deps.
Determinism strategy is comprehensive (C14 has 6 distinct cross-process byte-equality checks). Fixed-timestamp injection points are named for chronicle, leaderboard, and summary.
Stable seed-from-string algorithm is pinned to SHA-256 (not Python's salted hash()), with explicit cross-process determinism in C7/C14.
Badge format is exactly specified including a regex (^WHISPERDEEP seed=\d+ floors=\d+ turns=\d+ archetype=\w+ v1 [0-9a-f]{6}$) — testable mechanically.
Leaderboard semantics (sort-by-score-desc, tiebreak-by-timestamp-asc, cap at 50, missing/malformed file tolerance) are fully spelled out (C8).
Conflict detection for --seed / --daily / --seed-string is explicit (C10) — not just 'works' but also 'fails clearly'.
C15 (edge cases) covers five concrete scenarios that proposals often skip: empty seed-string, unwritable leaderboard path, duplicate keybind keys, archetype=None, audio=log without whisperer.
C20 final smoke test exercises every Sprint-12 surface in a single subprocess run, then re-runs to verify accumulation — strong end-to-end coverage.
Regression criterion C16 explicitly enumerates prior CLI flags AND verifies EVENT_TYPES has exactly the 10 known values (no accidental additions in Sprint 12).
Documentation criterion C18 is unusually concrete (six named subsections + README + deferred-sprints note + default keybind table).
weaknesses: C15 mixes two acceptable behaviors ('exit 0 with warning OR exit non-zero with clear error') for the unwritable-leaderboard case. This is documented-choice flexibility, but the evaluator must read the docstring at test time to pick the right assertion. Consider: tighten on the second contract pass if generator picks one.
C2 similarly allows 'raises ValueError OR returns DEFAULTS with a documented warning' for malformed JSON. Same flexibility risk as above. Acceptable for sprint-12 polish work; not blocking.

C6 step 4 says '[--audio null --dump-audio writes []] OR omits writing — choose and document one.' This is an open design choice masquerading as a test step. Generator should pick before submission, but the criterion permits either. Not blocking.

C13 (interactive loop dispatcher) is the trickiest to test in unit-form because the existing run_interactive blocks on stdin. The proposal asks for a 'small testable helper dispatch_command(game, kb, line)'; this is good, but the criterion does not explicitly REQUIRE such a helper to be public — only 'or equivalent'. If the generator inlines all dispatch logic, C13 may be hard to test cleanly. Recommend the generator expose dispatch_command as a public function.
C17 mandates >= 18 distinct test functions for a sprint with 15 testable feature criteria — appropriate, but on the lower bound given how much surface area Sprint 12 ships. Not a defect.

The chronicle 'daily: true' metadata field (C10) is mentioned but not separately tested — verification is implicit in the chronicle inspection. Consider: a future round could add an explicit metadata-line check, but it is in-scope of C10 step 2 indirectly.

coverageCheck.F12_keybinds_help: FULLY COVERED by C1 (module shape), C2 (config round-trip), C3 (help overlay), C4 (CLI --keys / --list-bindings), C13 (interactive dispatcher + :commands), C18 (docs), C19 (layering).
coverageCheck.F13_audio: FULLY COVERED by C5 (module shape + adapters + CUES + EVENT_TO_CUE), C6 (--audio + --dump-audio CLI + cue routing), C15a (audio log without whisperer edge case), C18 (docs), C19 (layering — no real audio libs).
coverageCheck.F14_daily_leaderboard_badge_summary: FULLY COVERED by C7 (leaderboard module), C8 (read/append/cap), C9 (CLI), C10 (--daily / --daily-date / --seed-string), C11 (build_badge + sibling .badge.txt), C12 (build_run_summary + --summary), C18 (docs).
coverageCheck.regression: C16 covers Sprints 1, 2, 7, 8, 10, 11 (all in-tree sprints). EVENT_TYPES verified exact. TileKind verified unchanged. All prior CLI flags listed.
coverageCheck.determinism: C14 covers all five deterministic-input modes cross-process.
coverageCheck.layering_and_codequality: C19 enforces import cleanliness on all four new modules + events.py + llm.py.

regressionSprintsCheck: regressionSprints=[1,2,7,8,10,11] correctly matches the in-tree completed sprints per handoff.json + sprint-11 eval. Sprints 3/4/5/6/9 are not in tree and correctly excluded. APPROVED.
outOfScopeCheck: Out-of-scope list correctly excludes: real audio backends (deferred), network leaderboard sync, save/load (Sprint 6), FOV/combat (Sprint 3), items (Sprint 4), monsters (Sprint 5), director nudges (Sprint 9), death-legends cross-run (preserved deferral from Sprint 10), GUI rebinder, multi-user accounts, mouse/gamepad. APPROVED.
suggestedRevisions: Non-blocking: Generator should resolve the three 'choose one and document' decisions before implementation: (a) malformed keybinds JSON behavior (C2), (b) --audio null --dump-audio empty-vs-no-write (C6), (c) unwritable leaderboard path behavior (C15). Documenting these in module docstrings up front prevents test churn.
Non-blocking: Recommend the generator expose dispatch_command (or run_one_command) as a PUBLIC function in cli.py to make C13 cleanly unit-testable without subprocess + stdin gymnastics.
verdict: ACCEPTED. Proposal is ready for the generator. Twenty criteria provide comprehensive coverage of all three features with strong determinism, layering, and regression guards. Polish-sprint scope is appropriate for the FINAL sprint of the project.
